preamble: The shift from [ei] to [ai] involves the lowering of the first element of the diphthong. This is a common development in many languages, often associated with vowel lowering shifts or chain shifts.

examples.note: Modern Dutch sociolinguistic variation involving the lowering of the nucleus.
Variation in several accents (e.g., Australian, Cockney) involving further lowering.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
